steps.title: Find the subject
steps.content: The sentence is: "Henry's careful construction ______ apparent." The subject is "Henry's careful construction" — a singular noun phrase. "Construction" is the head noun; "Henry's careful" are modifiers.

steps.title: Match verb to singular subject
steps.content: Rule: a linking verb must agree with its subject in number. The subject "construction" is singular, so the verb must be third-person singular present. A. "have become" — plural present perfect ("have" marks plural). Fails: "construction have become" violates agreement. B. "are becoming" — plural present progressive ("are" marks plural). Fails: "construction are becoming" violates agreement. C. "becomes" — third-person singular present (-s ending). Correctly matches the singular subject "construction." Correct. D. "become" — base/plural form. Fails: used with plural subjects or after "to" (infinitive), not with a singular noun subject. That matches choice C — C is correct.
